title: The Time I Lost It

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>I screamed at Sofia once. <break time="350ms"/>Really screamed. <break time="300ms"/>Like something came out of me that I didn't recognize.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>She was five. <break time="300ms"/>It was nothing. <break time="250ms"/>Spilled juice on my laptop. <break time="200ms"/>An accident. <break time="400ms"/>But I hadn't slept. <break time="350ms"/>The baby was colicky. <break time="300ms"/>I was running on nothing. <break time="500ms"/>And I screamed. <break time="400ms"/>Her face crumpled. <break time="350ms"/>She ran to her room crying. <break time="500ms"/>I stood there shaking. <break time="400ms"/>More scared of myself than I'd ever been. <break time="350ms"/>That rage - <break time="300ms"/>it came from somewhere deep. <break time="250ms"/>Somewhere exhausted and empty.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>I apologized. <break time="400ms"/>Got down on her level. <break time="350ms"/>Told her mommy made a mistake. <break time="300ms"/>That it wasn't about the juice. <break time="500ms"/>Kids can understand repair. <break time="400ms"/>What they need to see is that we're human. <break time="350ms"/>That we mess up. <break time="300ms"/>And that we come back and make it right. <break time="500ms"/>That moment scared me into getting help. <break time="400ms"/>Therapy. <break time="350ms"/>More support. <break time="300ms"/>It was the wake-up call.

when_to_share: When a parent admits to losing control or having a rage moment
